summary: Hardened the governed Coolify repo-deploy wrapper with bounded retries for transient registry failures, corrected the generated Coolify app edge upstream to HTTPS `443`, replayed the NGINX edge and scoped guest-network policy, and restored healthy public reachability for `education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org` while recording that fresh rebuilds from upstream `main` still fail on repeated Docker Hub token timeouts.

verification.check: Infra repo baseline before live work
verification.observed: `git fetch origin` confirmed local `main` and `origin/main` both at `c4e6619a7b4e922a35600240dd8da9f14d00191f` before the follow-up work began.
The companion repository `baditaflorin/education_wemeshup` upstream `main` resolved to `d6c58dd69f8dbeaa7e177c6edd8c6a4526118f41` after merge of PR `#2` and PR `#3`.

`uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python -m pytest tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py tests/test_coolify_tool.py tests/test_lv3_cli.py -q` passed with `72 passed in 6.04s` after adding transient deployment retry classification plus the `coolify_apps` HTTPS upstream generation fix.

`python3 scripts/coolify_tool.py deploy-repo --repo git@github.com:baditaflorin/education_wemeshup.git --branch main --source private-deploy-key --app-name education-wemeshup --project 'LV3 Apps' --environment production --build-pack dockercompose --docker-compose-location compose.yaml --compose-domain catalog-web=education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org --ports 80 --description 'education_wemeshup production update to latest main' --force --wait --timeout 1800 --max-deploy-attempts 5 --retry-delay 5` retried five times and exposed the same classified failure on every attempt: `docker-registry-auth-timeout` while fetching Docker Hub tokens for base images such as `alpine`, `node`, and `nginxinc/nginx-unprivileged`.

`uv run --with pyyaml python3 scripts/generate_platform_vars.py --write` now renders `coolify_apps.edge.upstream` and `coolify_apps.urls.internal` as `https://10.10.10.70:443` instead of the stale plain-HTTP `:80` contract.

`make configure-edge-publication EXTRA_ARGS='--limit nginx-lv3 -e public_edge_sync_generated_static_dirs=false'` completed with `nginx-lv3 ok=60 changed=3 failed=0`, reloading the generated NGINX public-edge configuration against the corrected Coolify HTTPS upstream.

verification.check: Scoped guest-network replay for Coolify HTTPS ingress
verification.observed: A scoped `playbooks/guest-network-policy.yml` replay for `proxmox_florin,coolify-lv3` completed with `proxmox_florin ok=22 changed=3 failed=0` and `coolify-lv3 ok=13 changed=0 failed=0`, reinstating the Proxmox guest-firewall path used by `nginx-lv3` to reach Coolify on TCP `443`.

verification.check: Public application reachability after edge and firewall replay
verification.observed: `curl -ksSI --max-time 10 --resolve education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org/` returned `HTTP/2 200`, and `curl -ksS --max-time 10 --resolve education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org/api/v1/catalog/taxonomy | jq '.meta.apiVersion, .meta.categoryCount, .meta.activityCount'` returned `"v1"`, `2`, and `6`.

notes: The current codified non-chat operator path is already present in repo form through `make coolify-manage ...` and `python3 scripts/lv3_cli.py deploy-repo ...`; the intended future browser surface remains the ADR 0093 ops portal calling the same governed contract.

The live public app is healthy after the edge and firewall replay, but the platform still needs one more hardening step to make repeated fresh repo pulls routine at scale: a more reliable container-registry path than anonymous Docker Hub token fetches during Coolify rebuilds.
The public frontend currently serves asset names `assets/index-CoqZn72J.js` and `assets/index-QCQcV1UB.css`, which match the current upstream `main` frontend build output.
